result.summary: The page is largely functional-looking, but several elements are visually truncated or clipped at the viewport edges, and the bottom navigation bar overlaps content. Most issues relate to layout clipping that may or may not be intentional carousel behavior, but some appear genuinely broken.

issues.description: The 'Hot Games' navigation chip in the top category bar is cut off, displaying only 'Hot Gar' with the rest clipped by the viewport edge with no visible scroll affordance.
issues.suggestion: Add a visual scroll indicator (fade gradient or partial peek styling) or adjust chip sizing so users understand the row scrolls horizontally; ensure the label isn't hard-clipped mid-word.

issues.description: The last row of game tiles ('Who Wants to Be a Millionaire', 'Rakin Bacon', 'Dark Waters') is abruptly cut off by the fixed bottom navigation bar, hiding parts of the tiles and their info buttons.
issues.suggestion: Add bottom padding to the scrollable content area equal to the height of the fixed bottom nav so content is never obscured behind it.

issues.description: Game tiles in the rightmost column ('Magnificent Phoenix', 'Munchie Mil...', 'Hyperc...') are clipped at the screen edge, cutting off titles and jackpot info.
issues.suggestion: If these rows are horizontal carousels, this may be intentional peek behavior, but consider showing a cleaner partial-tile crop or scroll arrows to make scrollability obvious.

issues.description: Promo banner text on the left arrow side is tight against the carousel arrow, and the left chevron arrow overlaps the banner's edge with low contrast against the yellow background.
issues.suggestion: Increase contrast of the carousel navigation arrows or add a subtle background behind them so they remain visible over light banner imagery.

issues.description: The 'i' info buttons on game tiles are small dark squares that may be hard to tap accurately and have low contrast over dark tile artwork (e.g., on 'Cop the Lot').
issues.suggestion: Increase the tap target to at least 44x44px and ensure sufficient contrast between the icon and its background across all tile artworks.
